title: audioinfo
summary: Read metadata from an audio file.

gpu_support.notes: Runs on the CPU. Audio metadata inspection reads container headers and does not involve acceleration providers.

description: `info = audioinfo(filename)` returns a MATLAB-style metadata structure for supported audio containers.
behaviors: Accepts scalar string, scalar string-array, or row character-vector filenames and reads through RunMat's filesystem abstraction.
Recognizes WAV/RF64 RIFF containers, FLAC STREAMINFO metadata, AIFF/AIFC COMM metadata, Ogg/Vorbis identification headers, and MPEG audio frame headers.
`TotalSamples`, `Duration`, `BitsPerSample`, or `BitRate` are `NaN` when the container does not expose enough information in headers alone.
For MP3 files, metadata is estimated from the first valid MPEG audio frame header after an optional ID3v2 tag. Variable-rate files may require future decoder support for exact duration.
Malformed or unsupported audio files raise a descriptive format error rather than returning partial metadata.

examples.description: Use duration and total sample count

faqs.question: Does `audioinfo` decode audio samples?
faqs.answer: No. It reads container metadata only. Sample decoding belongs to `audioread`.

faqs.question: Which formats are supported?
faqs.answer: WAV/RF64, FLAC, AIFF/AIFC, Ogg/Vorbis, and MPEG audio frame headers, including common MP3 files.

faqs.question: Why are some fields `NaN`?
faqs.answer: Some compressed formats do not expose exact sample count, bit depth, or duration in their primary headers. RunMat preserves the field shape and uses `NaN` for unavailable metadata.

gpu_residency: `audioinfo` is a host file-I/O operation and returns a host struct. No GPU residency is created or consumed.
gpu_behavior: `audioinfo` does not launch provider kernels or participate in fusion. It reads audio container headers from the active filesystem provider and constructs a metadata struct on the host.
